# Mantis

Mantis extracts readable content from the rendered browser DOM and converts it to structured JSON
or clean Markdown. `Mantis.extract(document)` returns the canonical article object;
`Mantis.toMarkdown(article, { frontmatter, images, tables, maxChars, budget })` renders
token-efficient Markdown with inline links, emphasis, nested lists, and fenced code;
`budget: "outline"` makes an over-budget page degrade like an outline (headings and section leads
survive first) instead of losing its tail. `Mantis.fromHTML(html, { url, DOMParser })` is the
server-side entry point (inject jsdom or linkedom). Frontmatter carries url, confidence,
contentHash, and warnings so agents can branch on extraction quality and dedupe.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis#readme): API overview, AI agent patterns (Playwright, jsdom), bookmarklet helper, and development commands.
- [Source](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/mantis.js): Runtime library entry point.
- [Types](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/mantis.d.ts): TypeScript API declarations.
- [Tests](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/test.js): Contract coverage for extraction, structured output, Markdown, HTML, and bookmarklet behavior.
- [Benchmarks](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/benchmark.js): Fixed fixture evaluation for extraction and Markdown quality.
- [Perf](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/perf.js): Fixed-metric render-path harness for toMarkdown.
- [Demo](https://github.com/yrstm/mantis/blob/main/demo/server.js): Local bookmarklet receiver for trying Mantis in a browser.

## Package

- CommonJS entry: `mantis.js`
- Runtime dependencies: none
